title: LLMs
intro: Foundational model families grouped by licensing and accessibility. The appendix treats this as a curated snapshot rather than an exhaustive catalog.

items.name: GPT series
items.description: A series of powerful and widely used LLMs from OpenAI.
features: State-of-the-art performance across a wide range of natural language tasks.
Strong reasoning, coding, and creative text generation capabilities.
items.models: GPT-4, GPT-4o, GPT-5, GPT-5.5, o1, o3, and various fine-tuned versions.
items.license: Proprietary, accessible via a paid API.

items.name: Claude series
items.description: A family of conversational AI models from Anthropic known for focusing on safety and producing helpful and honest responses.
features: Large context windows for processing and analyzing long documents.
Strong performance in conversational and text-processing tasks.
Emphasis on constitutional AI and safety.
items.license: Proprietary, accessible via a paid API.

items.name: Gemini series
items.description: A family of multimodal LLMs developed by Google DeepMind, designed to process and understand various data types, including text, images, audio, video, and code.
features: Natively multimodal capabilities, allowing for joint understanding of different data types.
Advanced reasoning and coding abilities.
Substantial context windows reaching up to 1 million tokens in Gemini 1.5 models.
Optimized versions for different applications, including Ultra for complex tasks, Flash for real-time interactions, and Nano for on-device use.
items.models: Gemini Ultra, Gemini Pro, Gemini Flash, and Gemini Nano, with various versions such as 1.0, 2.0, 3.0, and 3.5.

items.name: Gemma
items.description: A family of lightweight, state-of-the-art open models from Google, built from the same research and technology used to create the Gemini models.
features: High performance and efficiency, with smaller models often outperforming larger ones.
Strong focus on responsible AI development.
items.license: Gemma license, which allows commercial use under its terms.

items.name: Llama
items.description: A family of open-weight LLMs from Meta, including the latest Llama 5 with multimodal capabilities.
features: Llama 5 introduces a mixture-of-experts (MoE) architecture and can process text, images, and video.
Very long context windows that can handle up to 10 million tokens in Llama 5 Scout.
A community license that allows for free use and modification up to a certain scale.

items.name: DeepSeek
items.description: High-performance, cost-efficient LLMs from DeepSeek AI, with a strong focus on coding and reasoning.
features: Advanced reasoning and efficient hardware utilization.
A MoE architecture for enhanced performance.
Open-weight models that can be run locally.
items.models: The DeepSeek-V3 and DeepSeek-R1 series, as well as specialized models for vision (DeepSeek-VL2), coding (DeepSeek-Coder), and math (DeepSeek-Math).
items.license: A dual model where the code is under the MIT license and the models are under the DeepSeek License.

items.name: Mistral AI
items.description: AI startup that develops a high-performance range of open-weight and proprietary models.
features: Some models are released under specific non-commercial licenses.
Proprietary models are accessible via a paid API.
items.models: Open-weight models such as Mistral 7B and Mistral 8x7B, and proprietary models such as Mistral Small, Mistral Medium, and Mistral Large.
items.license: A mix of licenses, including Apache 2.0 for some models and proprietary licenses for others.

items.name: Qwen
items.description: A comprehensive family of LLMs from Alibaba Cloud.
features: Many models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license.
Alibaba Cloud's most advanced models are kept proprietary.
items.license: A mix of Apache 2.0 for many models and a proprietary license for the most advanced ones.

items.name: Grok
items.description: LLM from xAI, with a focus on real-time information access.
features: Grok-1 was released as open-source.
Newer and more powerful versions, such as Grok-2, Grok-3, and Grok-5 are proprietary.
Access to proprietary models is available through paid subscriptions on the X platform and via an API.
items.models: Grok-1 (open-source), Grok-2, Grok-3, Grok-5, and subsequent versions (proprietary).
items.license: A hybrid approach where Grok-1 is under the Apache 2.0 license, and newer models are proprietary.

items.name: Kimi K2.6
items.description: A trillion-parameter open-weight language model from Moonshot AI designed for high-end reasoning, tool-use, and agentic workflows.
features: MoE architecture with approximately 1 trillion total parameters and 32 billion active per token.
Very long context window support, including up to 256k tokens in the Thinking variant.
Agent capabilities such as tool invocation, chain of thought, and sustained workflows.
items.license: Modified MIT license allowing open-weight distribution under model-specific terms.
